story.title: As a user I want to sort products by name and price
story.description: On the Saucedemo inventory page, the product sort control orders products by name (A to Z, Z to A) and by price (low to high, high to low), and the chosen sort option stays visible as the active selection. Catalog component; client-side sort (Saucedemo has no backend sort API). Fetched from Jira SK-13 (Mode B).

acceptance_criteria: sorting A to Z works correctly
sorting Z to A works correctly
sorting low to high works correctly
sorting high to low works correctly

ambiguities.description: The Jira AC is one compound sentence; it has been split into five discrete entries (four sort modes + the selected-option-visible check). Confirm the split preserves intent.

Saucedemo performs sorting client-side; there is no backend API that accepts a sort parameter. So this story is E2E-only — there is no API contract to assert at a lower level. (The API branch of the pipeline was exercised separately by STORY-002 against reqres.in.) Confirm E2E-only is the right scope.

'works correctly' is interpreted as: after selecting a sort mode, the visible product order matches that ordering (names alphabetical asc/desc; prices numeric asc/desc). Confirm this operational definition.

risks.description: A selected sort produces the wrong order (e.g. price low-to-high actually shows high-to-low, or name sort is not alphabetical), so the user cannot find products by their intended ordering.
Price sorting compares prices as strings rather than numbers (e.g. $10 sorts before $9), giving a plausible-looking but incorrect order.
After choosing a sort option the control does not reflect the active selection, so the user cannot tell which sort is applied.

requirements_reviewed.notes: Gate 1 approved. ACs match SK-13 verbatim (5-way split confirmed). All 3 ambiguities accepted: E2E-only scope (Saucedemo client-side sort, no backend API), the AC split, and the 'works correctly' = visible-order-matches-ordering definition.

test_scope_reviewed.notes: Gate 2 approved. 5 TCs, all approved. Granular per-sort-mode cases kept for failure diagnosability; price sorts P0 with numeric-parse assertions (RISK-002). 100% E2E correct for client-side sort.

specs_reviewed.notes: Gate 3 approved. 5 scenarios = 5 approved TCs; sort behavior + active-option locator verified live; price sorts assert numeric order (RISK-002); no unrelated flows.

code_reviewed.notes: Gate 4 approved. 5 tests, stable [data-test] locators, web-first assertions (lint enforced the more robust form), numeric price comparison guards RISK-002; suite passes live, lint+typecheck clean.
